item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's an interesting skip, but I'm not sure how I feel about the ending. I like the Greek myth stuff peppered into it, but there are a few things that I think could be clarified or strengthened.

How did -F and -G get in there? Is it actually them, or their Oneiric forms? If -A was recruiting them, how did -G get in there? I feel like a decent part of the impact of the ending depends on the reader's implicit understanding of the Oneiroi Collective mythology, and would benefit more from elaboration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get it. OK, film studio in a dream, and DVDs resulting from it, that far is fine. But then, the wake of the experiment 06 beats me. So, first -G sort of takes over? And then gets Caught up in Agent Mixon? If so:

  • Why does -G take over after a film about the dream studio is made?
  • How did Mixon recover afterwards?

(I believe I understand that -G ended up in Mixon simply because it attacked her / him first and was sort of ripped out of the dream world when Mixon woke up.)

I assume that there is a connection with the murder case mentioned at the end, but for me this adds nothing to the SCP. My personal taste, probably - I am all for bare bones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: This user is permanently banned for vehemence and harassment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is seriously over-egged, and would benefit hugely from a thorough streamlining. Perhaps I'm missing something, but it felt like two or three unconnected stories running in parallel without ever meeting, even at a thematic level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to the safe nature of the object, also volunteers have also been accepted for these experiments.

Is this a typo or am I missing something?

Other than that this article is very good!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The interview was highly informal. Dr.Corvino had just the vague idea of what he wanted to do (a, interview movie) but past that he just went with what he felt. Not being clinical at all, just acting as a normal interviewer.

About the reading, I would just assume that the task force's member dreamers don't work like normal dreaming people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh, i might have missed that guide, clearly! Thank you for the suggestion. and about your ideas… maybe. just keep an eye out for the delivery service.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I've had dreams in which I were basically reading things written in papers. A teacher I once had told me that, when the children used to read more, they commonly dreamt with written words (he did). I think it's posible to read in dreams.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Possible) spoilers
- You've been warned

The Greek guy is Morpheus? I had that idea when Francis Black said he was Greek, and the references to greek mythology you made later made me think it wasn't absurd.

And, maybe I'm overthinking the plot, but can I suppouse 3381-F is Susan Baker and she's happy because 3381-G (her murderer) has left that onirical world and he can't frighten her any longer?

A really interesting SCP. I enjoyed the reading, take my +1.

I suggest you make a small change:

All testing on SCP-3381 must be approved by Dr. Corvino

According to the guides, the procedures shouldn't mention specific persons, because they can move to another project / die in a containment breach / retire, and in that eventuality the staff wouldn't know who they've to contact. You can replace "Dr. Corvino" for "the main researcher", or something similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really interesting concept! I think it would be good of closing a few minor loopholes however:

- When (non-lucid) dreaming, things like interviews prepared while awake are hard to do, since the dreamer might not even remember anything about their awakened self.

- You can't actually read in dreams if I remember correctly. You can use dream logic to your advantage here however; the dreamer can magically know that this is a movie studio and never question why they actually know this.

Would it be better for the narrative consistency if the dreams while using the SCP are always lucid?

Apart from that, well done! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, first gander in a longer format, plus a first try at interviews. Should be decent enough, at least on a grammar level… Ah, and I almost forgot, the picture was made by me, it is mine, and i do allow myself to use it.
